widgets_values: CCSR model used for CCSR. Get it on their github page.
Limit size to 4096 on the longest edge - 4k.
Get filename from input file. This will be used for the output files.
Display input image dimensions for reference.

SUPIR Sampler

Restore EDM sampler gives the best results in my testing. Even though the recommended settings for Realviz XL is 6 steps - it stills leaves a lot of noise. 24 steps gives the best quality.
Base model for SUPIR upscale. Realvis XL and Boltning works well. You can try other models.

You'll need the following upscale models to run this workflow:


4xRealWebPhoto_v4_dat2
https://openmodeldb.info/models/4x-RealWebPhoto-v4-dat2


OmniSR 4x DF2K
https://openmodeldb.info/models/4x-OmniSR-DF2K


4xNomos2_hq_dat2
https://openmodeldb.info/models/4x-Nomos2-hq-dat2


NMKD Superscale 8x
https://icedrive.net/s/43GNBihZyi

Tiled diffusion methods tends to create color shifts. We want our colors to be consistent. This node fixes the colors.

No need to upscale the color reference image, the node already does this internally.

sampling methods:

ccsr = normal, untiled, heavy on VRAM

ccsr_tiled_mixdiff = tiled, each tile decoded separately, has seams

ccsr_tiled_vae_gaussian_weights = seams fixed, can be less accurate and introduce extra noise

There is a resolution to upscale factor relationship in CCSR.

If your input image is in the SDXL resolution range, a factor of 2 - 3 is the sweet spot. At a factor of 4, you'll start getting ugly details.

If your input image is over 2K, an upscale of 4 works best.
